query: map vs object javascript interview question

competitors.label: MDN Map
competitors.selectionReason: MDN is the canonical reference for Map semantics, key types, iteration, and lookup behavior.
theirStrengths: It is authoritative on Map behavior, insertion order, and the API surface.
theirGaps: It is less direct on the interview decision boundary of when an Object is still the better modeling tool.

realWorldUseCases: Use Map when you need a true key-value collection with non-string keys and predictable iteration. Use a plain Object when modeling a fixed record with known string keys.
actionableExamples: Map is purpose-built for dynamic key-value data with any key type and stable iteration.
followUpConfusion: Objects are best for structured records and JSON-shaped data.

realWorldUseCases: MDN explains Map correctly, but our page is more explicit about the architecture choice between dynamic collections and record-shaped objects.
actionableExamples: The canonical docs still provide strong examples of Map usage, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our page answers the likely follow-up confusion more directly: Map is not automatically better, because Objects still fit records, JSON payloads, and fixed shapes better.

actionableExamples: Add one short object-key coercion example later if we need more example distance over the docs.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical Map reference, but our page is stronger for this prompt because it turns the API difference into a clearer modeling decision.
